Mike, his partner, told me yesterday that he probably has read your book, The Oldroom Marketing Plan, quite literally a dozen times. When I first joined up with Mike, it was the first book he had me read. He has four copies of the book himself, the first copy purchased in 1998, and it has notes and highlighting. So he's flattering, he's selling me on why I should take the speaking gig. So he's already hired me in his head the time we get here. Now I actually wanted this thing for some reason, and I can't remember now why I did. So we, I must have had Vicki do this, but we got, before I got on the phone with him, so we scheduled a phone appointment with him. And then I had, we got Bill Hammond, who's, who markets two lawyers and would be known by this guy to quickly write and fax over a testimonial letter to the guy about me. And Harry Williams, who's my coaching group, who's an attorney, fax over a letter, a testimonial to me. So by the time I got on the phone with the guy, he'd been sent a box. He'd been made to jump through the hoop, but write in the fax about why it is they wanted to hire me. And he'd gotten two faxes from two different lawyer guys telling them what a superstar I was. I can't remember now why I wanted the gig. I can't imagine why I wanted the gig. It wasn't Vegas. Maybe I just wanted to go to Vegas. I don't remember. Here's a consulting confirmation letter. This is a control paragraph. It's in a lot of stuff. You'll find a lot of stuff. The control statement is, consultant shall be available for telephone consulting regarding this project while the work is being completed. Subsequently, as long as clients testing or using work royalties are being paid under a reasonable man standard, meaning that such time and access must be limited to essential discussion. Client understands consultant is rarely available on short notice and messages or faxes may be answered after several days and telephone conferences may require advanced scheduling. Should additional in-person meetings be required by client, published hourly or daily fees will apply. So it's in the agreement. They signed the agreement. They got a copy of the agreement. If they later want to fuss about it, I got an agreement to pull out. You'll also find some language in these contracts about indemnity, meaning their responsibility, your responsibility, what happens if they get sued, if you get sued. Ownership of work language, that's in there. There's... Oh, this is kind of interesting. There's a clause I added recently because somebody managed to find another way to surprise me, which I had not anticipated and had never occurred before. They took the work I did for them to use in their business and they bundled it up and packaged it and sold it to a whole bunch of other people. So now we have a resale rights restriction. Under no circumstances is client authorized to create an information product for sale that is comprised entirely or primarily of the copy provided by consultant. Nor is client authorized to otherwise resell said copy via licensing publication shared use or other means or media. Copy is provided only and exclusively for use by client and client's own business. So every time somebody figures out some new way, my contract gets bigger. This is a good client who's screaming and screaming and screaming trying to get more phone time than is allocated to him by the program that he is in. So this is... And we're trying to accommodate him, but I only have like two days a month that I do phone appointments and so he would keep coming back to Vicki with, I can't do those two days, but here's eight days, see if Dan can do... So this is a letter explaining from me now. I know you were trying to get additional phone call time with me. The problem is I only set aside a couple days each month. Other days are dedicated to other purpose. So coming back with lists of days and times choices does not really work. I'm done for December and simply can't find room for an exception. I could have, but now I was punishing him for aggravating Vicki at the office by continuing to come back with stuff. Next day Vicki has to work with us January 18th and February 22nd. And so his behavior has changed and now he's fitting himself in where he should have fit himself in in the first place. I'll skip over that. There's a lot of client preparation stuff you will find in here. There's follow-up letters. There's fee rationale. Here's a little client management letter. Don't hunt for it because I've now skipped over a bunch of stuff. This paragraph. Written feedback is better than oral so I can make notes, copy and pass along. I got to keep training them to give me notes, not give me phone calls, not give me phone messages. I also put in Summers-Whites confirmation letter for you.
